scenarios.title: Production folder→topic resolution remains unchanged after the dead utility is removed

gherkin.given: the bookmarks package source after F-SIMPLIFY-R6-002 has been applied (FolderToTopicMapping function deleted from bookmarks.go and 3 dead tests deleted from bookmarks_test.go)
gherkin.when: grep is run for 'FolderToTopicMapping' across '*.go' files in internal/connector/bookmarks
gherkin.then: exactly zero matches are returned, TopicMapper.MapFolder remains the single production folder→topic entry point, and the full bookmarks-package test suite passes (44 PASS, 0 FAIL)

scenarios.title: Spec and design planning truth reflects the actual TopicMapper.MapFolder mechanism

gherkin.given: the spec 009 planning artifacts after F-SIMPLIFY-R6-002 has been applied (4 references in spec.md and 4 references in design.md rewritten to name TopicMapper.MapFolder)
gherkin.when: grep is run for 'FolderToTopicMapping' across 'specs/009-bookmarks-connector/spec.md', 'design.md', and 'scopes.md'
gherkin.then: exactly zero matches are returned; all eight rewritten reference sites name 'TopicMapper.MapFolder' or describe the split + per-segment exact/fuzzy/create resolution; historical spec 003 evidence rows referencing 'FolderToTopicMapping' are intentionally preserved untouched

scenarios.title: Adversarial guard test pins the split-not-flatten algorithmic intent

gherkin.given: the new TestSimplifyR6_FolderToTopicMapping_Removed test in topics_test.go with three layers of coverage (compile-pin via direct MapFolder call, runtime-pin via nil-pool assertion, and inline algorithmic-intent reproduction via the same strings.Split + non-empty-trim loop visible in topics.go::MapFolder)
gherkin.then: the test PASSES, directly calls TopicMapper.MapFolder with a nil pool to compile-pin the signature, reproduces the same strings.Split + non-empty-trim segmentation logic visible in topics.go::MapFolder for 6 paths including leading/trailing-slash edge cases, and the test's failure diagnostic names 'F-SIMPLIFY-R6-002 invariant' as the grep target for future reviewers
